instructions: Attempt all questions in Section A and Section C.
Attempt any three questions from Section B.

subquestions.text: In a daisy-chaining bus arbitration scheme, explain why the physical distance of a device from the bus controller creates a 'priority-starvation' scenario for downstream devices.
The IEEE 754 standard uses a biased exponent rather than a standard 2's complement representation. Justify this design choice in the context of floating-point comparison operations.
subquestions.topic: IEEE Standard for Floating Point Numbers

subquestions.text: A processor features a 4-stage pipeline. If a sequence of instructions contains heavy data dependencies, analyze why the actual speedup might drop significantly below the theoretical maximum of 4.

A software loop repeatedly accesses memory locations that map to the same set in a direct-mapped cache, causing 'thrashing'. Propose how changing the mapping to 2-way set associative would mitigate this.

During a high-speed data transfer from a disk drive, the CPU is frequently interrupted, leading to system lag. Explain how a Direct Memory Access (DMA) controller solves this by 'stealing' bus cycles.
Compare a stack-organized CPU with a general-purpose register-organized CPU. Which one would typically require fewer bits to represent an 'ADD' instruction, and why?

In Booth’s Multiplication Algorithm, if the current multiplier bit is 1 and the previous bit was 0 (bit pair 10), what specific arithmetic operation is performed on the partial product, and what is the logic behind it?

Design a 16-bit instruction format for a hypothetical processor that supports 32 general-purpose registers and 15 distinct operation codes. If the processor must support an 'Immediate' addressing mode for constants up to 255, evaluate if a single-word instruction is sufficient. Suggest an alternative if not.

A digital system employs a 2-level memory hierarchy with a main memory of 1 MB and a cache of 64 KB. If the cache uses 4-way set-associative mapping with a block size of 128 bytes, calculate the number of bits in the Tag, Set, and Word fields. Explain the 'Replacement Policy' conflict that arises when all lines in a set are full.

A real-time embedded system manages three I/O devices with varying urgencies. Describe the hardware implementation of a 'Priority Interrupt Controller' using a priority encoder. How does the CPU distinguish between a software exception and an external hardware interrupt during the fetch-execute cycle?

Analyze the architectural differences between a Synchronous and an Asynchronous bus. In a scenario where a fast CPU needs to communicate with a very slow legacy peripheral, evaluate which bus type would be more reliable and explain the 'Handshaking' protocol involved.
subquestions.topic: types of buses and bus arbitration

subquestions.text: Perform the multiplication of binary numbers (-9) and (+6) using Booth's Algorithm, showing every step of the register contents (AC, QR, Qn+1). Explain why Booth’s algorithm is considered superior to the hardware-based 'Shift and Add' method for signed operands.

sections.title: Section C
sections.header_notes: Attempt all questions. Choose one option from each question.

options.label: a
options.text: A pipelined processor encounters a 'Branch' instruction at stage 2. Evaluate the performance penalty if the pipeline is flushed vs. if 'Branch Prediction' is used. Describe the hardware requirements for a simple 1-bit dynamic branch predictor.

options.label: b
options.text: Contrast the design of a Hardwired Control Unit with a Microprogrammed Control Unit. For a high-performance RISC processor with a limited instruction set, justify which control unit design would be more appropriate for maximizing clock speed.

options.label: a
options.text: Design a 2D memory organization for a 4096 x 16 bit RAM. Explain the 'Selection Logic' and why a 2 1/2D organization might be preferred to reduce the complexity of the row and column decoders.

options.label: b
options.text: In a virtual memory system, a 'Page Fault' occurs. Detail the exact sequence of events the OS and hardware perform to resolve this. Analyze how a Translation Lookaside Buffer (TLB) minimizes the performance overhead of address translation.

options.label: a
options.text: Compare 'Programmed I/O' with 'Interrupt-Initiated I/O'. In a system where the CPU is 1000 times faster than the I/O device, calculate the percentage of CPU time wasted in a polling loop and justify the transition to an interrupt-driven model.

options.label: b
options.text: Explain the concept of 'I/O Channels'. Differentiate between a Selector Channel and a Multiplexer Channel. Which one would you implement for a system managing multiple low-speed terminals versus a single high-speed magnetic tape drive?

options.label: a
options.text: Identify the role of 'Addressing Modes' in improving code density. For a task involving the traversal of a large 2D array, justify why 'Base-Register' plus 'Indexed' addressing is superior to absolute addressing.

options.label: b
options.text: A system uses a centralized bus arbiter with a 'Rotating Priority' (Round Robin) scheme. Analyze how this prevents device starvation compared to a 'Fixed Priority' scheme. Illustrate the control signals (Bus Request, Bus Grant) involved in one arbitration cycle.
options.topic: types of buses and bus arbitration

options.label: a
options.text: Design a 4-bit Look-Ahead Carry Adder (LACA) using Carry Generate (G) and Carry Propagate (P) functions. Mathematically demonstrate why the propagation delay of a LACA is independent of the number of bits compared to a Ripple Carry Adder.

options.label: b
options.text: Analyze the IEEE 754 Single Precision format for representing floating-point numbers. Convert the decimal value -25.75 into its 32-bit hexadecimal equivalent. Discuss how 'Underflow' is handled using subnormal numbers.
options.topic: IEEE Standard for Floating Point Numbers
